business.headline: 8th Pay Commission: OPS demand pending, but govt gives relief to these employeesThe government has said compassionate appointment cases filed by December 31, 2003, can be considered under the old pension rules even if the appointment came later. The clarification offers relief to affected employees while the wider OPS debate continues before the 8th Pay Commission.

Crude oil hits 4-month low. Will petrol and diesel prices fall?Crude oil prices fell to a near four-month low as Middle East tensions eased and supply prospects improved. The drop may help India's import bill, but petrol and diesel prices are unlikely to fall immediately.

Why many Indians earning Rs 1 lakh still struggle to saveFor many Indians, earning Rs 1 lakh a month once symbolised financial success. Yet, despite reaching this milestone, many professionals find themselves living pay cheque to pay cheque, with little left for savings. So where does all the money go?

Sebi looks to widen direct trading access; retail investors could benefitSebi has proposed allowing all categories of investors, including retail participants, to use Direct Market Access (DMA), a facility that enables orders to be placed directly into a stock exchange's trading system through a broker's infrastructure.

Sensex ends 893 points lower, Nifty below 24,000; Infosys falls 3%The S&P BSE Sensex dropped 893.39 points, or 1.16%, to close at 76,200.68, while the NSE Nifty50 fell 278.80 points, or 1.16%, to settle at 23,824.10.

Sensex drops over 900 points: Why is stock market falling today?Dalal Street fell sharply on Tuesday as an Asian market selloff and weak IT shares dragged benchmarks lower. The decline has shifted focus to US rate signals, monsoon worries and fading momentum after the recent rally.

Got your Form 16? Here's why Form 130 won't affect your ITR filing this yearConfused about Form 16 and Form 130? If you're preparing to file your ITR, here's why the upcoming change won't affect your return this year.

Iran wants to sell oil to India after US waiver. Tankers are nearby, but will India buy?Iran has begun pitching crude oil to Indian and other Asian refiners after the US waived sanctions for 60 days amid peace-deal negotiations. With Iranian tankers close to Indian shores, New Delhi appears to be an attractive market for Tehran, but will Indian refiners return to buying Iranian oil?

Pension complaint? Here are 5 ways PFRDA's new AI-powered platform can help youThe Pension Fund Regulatory and Development Authority has launched a new AI-powered platform called PFRDA Pension Sahayak, aimed at making pension grievance redressal faster, simpler and more accessible.

Seoul's Kospi crashes 10%, Japan's Nikkei falls 4%. Why are Asian markets down?Asian markets tumbled as investors sold tech and AI stocks, with South Korea's Kospi plunging 10% and Japan's Nikkei falling 3.6%.

Expecting an ITR deadline extension? Here's why it may not happen this yearMany taxpayers are hoping for another extension to the ITR filing deadline, as seen in previous years. However, this year's filing season appears different, and the chances of additional time may be lower than expected.

India-US trade talks enter final stretch ahead of July 24 deadlineUnion Minister Piyush Goyal and US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer are meeting in New Delhi to finalise the first phase of the India-US trade pact. The talks are significant as both sides seek tariff gains before the July 24 deadline.

SpaceX gets a reality check days after historic IPO listing, loses $600 billionLess than two weeks after making history, SpaceX is already confronting the burden of expectation. Investors have embraced Elon Musk's vision; now they want evidence that the business can deliver.

Sensex opens 170 points lower; Nifty tops 24,000; Infosys down 3%The S&P BSE Sensex fell 77.92 points, or 0.10%, to 77,016.15 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 slipped 18.90 points, or 0.08%, to 24,083.35.

SpaceX loses $600bn in 3 days, nearly 3 times Ambani and Adani's combined wealthThe wipeout is nearly three times the combined wealth of India's two richest businessmen — Reliance Industries Chairman Mukesh Ambani, whose net worth stands at $88.3 billion, and Adani Group Chairman Gautam Adani, whose fortune is estimated at $120 billion.

Meta, CRED and Kunal Shah: What's behind the $900 million deal?Meta's investment in CRED and its founder Kunal Shah has renewed interest in the fintech company's journey, business model and what lies ahead for one of India's most closely watched startups.

Tata Electronics hit by cyberattack; 2 lakh files linked to Apple, Tesla leakedThe breach is the latest setback for Apple's supply chain in India, where Tata faces scrutiny over alleged contamination of farmlands near one of its iPhone parts plants, Reuters reported.

8th Pay Commission in Lucknow: How much could salaries and pensions increase?With the 8th Pay Commission holding fresh talks in Lucknow, expectations around pay revisions and pension increases are once again in focus. The discussions could help shape the recommendations that follow.

Silver jumps Rs 5,000, gold up Rs 2,000: Time to buy or sell?Silver and gold prices have surged sharply today, sparking fresh interest among investors after recent volatility. So is this rally a buying opportunity or time to book profits?

Sensex closes 291 points higher, Nifty above 24,100; RIL up 1%The S&P BSE Sensex rose 291.17 points, or 0.38%, to close at 77,094.07, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 89.80 points, or 0.37%, to settle at 24,102.90.

entertainment.headline: Alka Yagnik's update on 'difficult health' after hearing loss: Making my way back Singer Alka Yagnik shared a health update after receiving the Padma Bhushan from President Droupadi Murmu. Her note said she is slowly finding her way back as she continues battling sensorineural nerve hearing loss following a viral attack.

Cocktail 2 vs Cocktail: Sicily, style and Shahid but where's the punch? Cocktail 2 may be creating waves in theatres, but despite its glossy visuals, picturesque locations and star-studded cast, it struggles to recreate the emotional depth and character-driven storytelling that made Cocktail such a memorable watch.

Akshay Kumar reveals his biggest career regret, says he wanted to slap himself Actor Akshay Kumar said he felt like slapping himself after spending a decade doing only action roles. The reflection framed his 35-year career as one of constant reinvention as Welcome to the Jungle nears release.

Why are the women of Toxic fading from the conversation as Yash takes centre stage? With Yash and Geetu Mohandas's Toxic now set for an August 26 release, promotional material increasingly revolves around Raya and Ticket. Is the absence of women deliberate?

Katrina Kaif or Vicky Kaushal? Sham Kaushal answers who grandson Vihaan resembles Veteran action director Sham Kaushal, in an interview, revealed who his grandson Vihaan looks like and talked about how becoming a grandfather was life-changing. He also spoke about Vicky's childhood, his family and why he has no plans to retire.

PM Modi meets Panchayat's fan-favourite duo Binod and Bhushan; reel goes viral Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Wednesday shared a light-hearted reel after meeting Panchayat actors Durgesh Kumar and Ashok Pathak on Tuesday. Titled 'A few moments with Binod', the clip quickly went viral on social media.

To every person who watched my film: Madhavan dedicates Padma Shri to fans, family Actor R Madhavan shared a post on social media after receiving the Padma Shri from President Droupadi Murmu. He said the honour deepened his sense of responsibility and thanked cinema, fans and family.

Tamannaah Bhatia discusses uncomfortable reality of women in South films, item songs Actor Tamannaah Bhatia has spoken about the differing expectations placed on women in Hindi and South cinema. She said commercial South films can be more restrictive, while defending dance numbers as enduring popular culture.

PM Modi meets Mammootty, family after Padma Bhushan honour Prime Minister Narendra Modi met actor Mammootty and his family after the Padma ceremony at Rashtrapati Bhavan. The Malayalam icon was honoured with Padma Bhushan in Delhi on June 23.

Naseeruddin Shah's Main Vaapas Aaunga era: 75, fearless, unmatched Naseeruddin Shah doesn't chase relevance; he defines it by showing up fully for stories that matter — poetic and personal. He quietly schools an entire industry on what sustained excellence looks like: fearless, and forever in demand. A glimpse at his versatility in Gustaakh Ishq, Made in India: A Titan Story and Main Vaapas Aaunga.

Landmark moment: Pregnant Deepika Padukone cheers for ICC's new maternity policy Deepika Padukone welcomed the ICC's new post-pregnancy guidelines for female cricketers. The actor is currently pregnant with her second child with husband, actor Ranveer Singh.

Alliance full contestants list: Daisy Shah, Ravi Kishan and all confirmed names Let the ultimate game of trust, power, and survival begin! Here's a list of the confirmed celebrities who are set to join Prime Video's upcoming show Alliance.

Nafisa Ali treks at 11,000 feet with familly and friends amid cancer recovery. Video Veteran actor Nafisa Ali Sodhi has shared videos from a trek at 11,000 feet with family and friends amid her cancer battle. The clips have renewed attention on her recovery journey and her earlier account of a delayed diagnosis.

Alka Yagnik gets love from fans as Padma Bhushan appearance sparks health concerns Alka Yagnik received the Padma Bhushan from President Droupadi Murmu at Rashtrapati Bhavan on June 23. The ceremony video prompted fans to send wishes as concern grew over her frail appearance.

Vijay makes 'it all worth it': Trisha's love-filled birthday post for CM After leaving fans wondering with her silence on June 22, actor Trisha Krishnan has finally posted a birthday note for Vijay. Sharing a heartfelt picture from the Tamil Nadu Chief Minister's late-night birthday celebrations, the actor's post comes amid rumours that the two had parted ways.

Meet Sukrut Deo: The breakout star of Latent 2 praised by Alia Bhatt, Samay Raina Comedian Sukrut Deo turned heads on India's Got Latent Season 2 with a viral act that earned praise from actor Alia Bhatt and show creator Samay Raina. His performance and emotional on-stage moments also resulted in a surge in his social media followers.

Eetha teaser X reactions: Fans crown Shraddha the face of women-led cinema The teaser of Eetha offers a first look at Shraddha Kapoor as Vithabai Bhau Mang Narayangaonkar. Fans reacted to the teaser and praised the film's rooted portrayal of Maharashtra's folk arts.

Appa was very angry: Shruti Haasan on her teenage act that angered Kamal Haasan Shruti Haasan revealed she has not visited a hair salon in five years and recalled how Kamal Haasan was "very, very angry" when she got a belly button piercing at 19.

Padma Awards 2026: Mammootty, Alka Yagnik to Madhavan, complete list of winners President Droupadi Murmu conferred actors Mammootty, R Madhavan and singer Alka Yagnik at the Padma Awards 2026 on Tuesday. The ceremony was held at the second Civil Investiture Ceremony in New Delhi.

general news.headline: Hoodie in June heat and phone left at office: How cops cracked Pune fort murderA CCTV camera installed near the fort's ticket counter captured a hooded man, later identified as Chetan Chaudhary, on June 18 trailing Pune businessman Ketan Agarwal and his fiancée Siya Goyal. Just hours later, Ketan was dead.

Mumbai wakes up to waterlogging after heavy overnight rain as monsoon hits cityHeavy rain left key parts of Mumbai waterlogged, including Dadar station, after the city recorded over 300 mm of rainfall in 24 hours. The rainfall falls in the IMD's "extremely heavy" category, highlighting the intensity of the monsoon downpour.

Stolen passport, cancelled Bali trip: Pune woman's murder plan began weeks earlierPolice allege Siya Goyal stole and misplaced fiance Ketan Agrawal's passport before a Bali trip. Investigators now see the incident as an early step in the murder conspiracy case.

Iran was on the ropes: Trump lashes out after US Senate votes to halt warUS President Donald Trump criticised the Senate after it passed a resolution seeking to limit further military action against Iran without congressional approval. The vote exposed Republican divisions and raised questions over Washington's leverage as talks with Tehran continue.

The Indian Political League auction: When defections become the new normalThe public spectacle of defections in Delhi and Maharashtra highlights how political realignments have evolved from exceptions into a normalised mechanism for expanding power.

Karnataka BJP weighs future of JDS alliance after cross-voting shock in MLC pollsThe cross-voting in the Karnataka MLC polls has raised questions within the BJP leadership regarding the alliance with the JD(S), the party's relevance in the state's political landscape and the saffron side's long-term strategy in the state.

Who are Nihangs? Sikh saint-warriors associated with weapons, horses, and controversiesA handful of Nihang Sikhs captured a Rudraprayag gurdwara and engaged in a stand-off with the Uttarakhand administration. The blue-robed Nihangs are associated with war, weapons, horses, cannabis, and controversies. What is their history, and who controls them? It's all explained here.

Cristiano Ronaldo, scrutiny's favourite child, answers with grace and a braceCristiano Ronaldo has spent 23 years turning doubt into fuel, and Houston offered another chapter in that story. Written off after DR Congo, the Portugal captain responded with a brace, a record and a reminder that criticism remains his favourite opponent.

BJP in, TMC out, Kolkata to rival Delhi NCR, Mumbai in race for airportsThe BJP-led government in West Bengal has announced plans for a greenfield airport near Kalyani, around 50 km north of Kolkata. Once operational, it will make Kolkata India's third metropolitan region with multiple commercial airports. Mamata Banerjee, as the CM, had resisted the Centre's plan for a second airport.

MP CM didn't buy land after taking office: CMO sources on suspicious deal claimA report claimed that Madhya Pradesh Chief Minister Mohan Yadav's family acquired large tracts of land in Ujjain that were expected to benefit from upcoming government infrastructure projects.

Women stabbed with syringes, raped: France's annual music fest turns nightmareThe French authorities arrested around 243 people following incidents of major chaos and violence in the annual Fête de la Musique celebrations.

Pune murder: 2 attempts, fiancée's birthday picnic ruse, and a mournful Insta postA cancelled Bali trip, an alleged failed murder attempt, a birthday picnic at a historic fort and a grieving Instagram post. Investigators say each piece formed part of a chilling plot that ended in death.

Cocktail 2 vs Cocktail: Sicily, style and Shahid but where's the punch?Cocktail 2 may be creating waves in theatres, but despite its glossy visuals, picturesque locations and star-studded cast, it struggles to recreate the emotional depth and character-driven storytelling that made Cocktail such a memorable watch.

BJP MLA's daughter tops CUET UG 2026, secures AIR 1Securing AIR 1 in CUET UG 2026, Delhi's former minister Kailash Gahlot's daughter said regular revision, conceptual clarity from NCERT and self-belief were central to her preparation strategy.

Cristiano Ronaldo ignores reporter's Messi question after Portugal brace. VideoFIFA World Cup 2026: Cristiano Ronaldo's record-breaking night for Portugal took an unexpected turn when he appeared to ignore a question about Lionel Messi. The brief interaction quickly went viral, reigniting discussion around football's most iconic rivalry.

40 drown in France trying to cool off as Europe bakes under record heatA severe heatwave sweeping across Europe has turned deadly, with France reporting 40 drowning deaths in just five days as people sought relief from soaring temperatures. Record heat has disrupted transport, forced school closures and shut major tourist sites, including the Eiffel Tower.

Bengaluru woman shares why working at Microsoft still feels rewarding after 5 yearsDeepika Koul shared a video documenting snippets from her daily life at Microsoft's Bengaluru office, offering a glimpse into the environment she credits for keeping her excited about work years into her career.

Video: Men smash smartphones after Haryana village's ban order to tackle cybercrimesA viral video from Sukhpuri village in Haryana's Nuh district showed some men smashing smartphones with stones as a symbolic gesture of cutting addiction and pledge to stay from crimes.

Fresh spell of monsoon rain across west coast and northeast on WednesdayThe IMD has forecast an active monsoon and rains across several regions, while some parts will continue to battle rising temperatures as monsoon remains on the way on June 24.

Pune woman who pushed fiancé off fort had made similar attempt 4 days earlierPolice are now examining allegations that Ketan Agarwal survived a first attempt on his life at Lohagad Fort on June 14 before returning to the same spot on June 18, where investigators allege he was pushed to his death.

Is Pakistan using Indus Valley to trick world on Indus treaty shelved by India?Pakistan is embracing the Indus Valley Civilisation as part of its national identity in a big way. It's a notable shift for a country that held the Sindh conquest of Muhammad bin Qasim in 712 CE as its foundational moment. So, why is Pakistan suddenly in love with Harappa and Mohenjo-daro? Does it have to do with the Indus Waters Treaty, which India has kept in abeyance?

No deal without nuclear inspections, Donald Trump warns Iran amid peace talksTrump said Iran would allow IAEA inspectors under the proposed US-Iran peace agreement. His remarks underscored a sharp dispute with Tehran over verification as negotiations continued.

After Lucknow inferno, checks at UP's exam hubs expose major safety lapsesAfter the Lucknow fire killed 15 people, authorities inspected coaching centres across Uttar Pradesh and found serious safety lapses. The checks have led to notices, sealing drives and fresh questions over student safety at major education hubs.

We conducted a fuel efficiency test of E85. Here's what we foundWith E85 fuel priced nearly Rs 20 lower per litre than E20, it appears to be a bargain. However, ethanol's lower energy density also means higher fuel consumption. So, does the lower pump price actually translate into lower running costs? We put the Suzuki Gixxer SF flex fuel through a mileage test to find out.

sports.headline: Jeremy Doku returns to Belgium squad after son's birth Jeremy Doku is set to rejoin Belgium after leaving camp for the birth of his son. His absence sparked debate, but teammates backed him before a crucial match against New Zealand.

Neymar fit and available but not certain to play vs Scotland, says Ancelotti Neymar is available for Brazil's final Group C clash against Scotland at the FIFA World Cup, but coach Carlo Ancelotti remained tight-lipped on whether the forward will feature in Miami after recovering from a calf injury.

Cristiano Ronaldo, scrutiny's favourite child, answers with grace and a brace Cristiano Ronaldo has spent 23 years turning doubt into fuel, and Houston offered another chapter in that story. Written off after DR Congo, the Portugal captain responded with a brace, a record and a reminder that criticism remains his favourite opponent.

Brave Panama's World Cup fight ends as Croatia stays alive with 1-0 win Ante Budimir's second-half header gave Croatia a 1-0 win over Panama in Toronto. The result kept Croatia alive in Group L and eliminated Panama from the FIFA World Cup 2026.

I don't deserve it: Jude Bellingham's blunt take on MOTM award after Ghana draw Jude Bellingham admitted he did not deserve the Man of the Match award after England's attacking struggles were exposed in a frustrating 0-0 draw against Ghana in Group L in Boston.

All-round Perry gets Australia ready for India tie with Pakistan hammering Ellyse Perry's 71 and 2/9 drove Australia to a 113-run win over Pakistan in Leeds. The result left Australia on the verge of the semi-finals before a high-stakes meeting with India at Lord's.

Second game syndrome hits England hard in lacklustre draw against Ghana England were held to a goalless draw by Ghana in Boston after failing to break down a deep defence. The result revived a familiar second-match problem for the Three Lions and raised questions on Thomas Tuchel's tactics in the contest.

France coach Didier Deschamps to miss Norway clash after mother's death FIFA World Cup 2026: France coach Didier Deschamps has returned home after the death of his mother and will miss Friday's World Cup match against Norway. Assistant Guy Stephan will take charge as France, already through, look to finish top of Group I.

Iran catch a break: US relaxes World Cup travel rules before Egypt clash FIFA World Cup 2026: The United States has allowed Iran to enter Seattle two days before its World Cup match against Egypt. The change gives Team Melli more preparation time, though post-match exit rules remain in place.

Cristiano Ronaldo ignores reporter's Messi question after Portugal brace. Video FIFA World Cup 2026: Cristiano Ronaldo's record-breaking night for Portugal took an unexpected turn when he appeared to ignore a question about Lionel Messi. The brief interaction quickly went viral, reigniting discussion around football's most iconic rivalry.

3 injured in stampede outside Ranchi stadium during Jharkhand T20 League A crowd surge at the West Gate before the Jharkhand T20 League final left spectators injured at Ranchi's JSCA Stadium on Tuesday, June 23.

Written off again, Cristiano Ronaldo answers with a brace as Portugal hit fiveFIFA World Cup 2026: Cristiano Ronaldo arrived in Houston surrounded by questions about age, relevance and whether Portugal had outgrown him. Ninety minutes later, he left with a brace, another World Cup record and a reminder that doubting him remains football's favourite mistake.

COL 1-0 COD Live: Munoz strikes late to put Colombia aheadColombia (COL) vs DR Congo (COD) live, FIFA World Cup 2026: COL 1-0 COD, Colombia moved closer to another important Group K win when Daniel Munoz broke the deadlock in the 76th minute after DR Congo goalkeeper Lionel Mpasi had kept them out with several fine saves. Colombia came into the match top of the group after beating Uzbekistan 3-1, while DR Congo had held Portugal to a draw in their opener.

Cristiano Ronaldo silences critics, becomes 1st player to score in 6 World CupsCristiano Ronaldo scored twice against Uzbekistan in Houston as Portugal took control. His opener made him the first player to score in six World Cups and answered criticism after the draw with DR Congo.

3,784 days of dominance: India beat Pakistan in 7-goal London hockey thrillerIndia edged Pakistan 4-3 in a chaotic FIH Pro League clash in London. The win stretched India's unbeaten run against Pakistan to 3,784 days despite a frantic late fightback.

Tejaswin Shankar gets AFI exemption, stays in Asian Games selection raceAFI has granted Tejaswin Shankar exemption from the National Inter-State Championships and will still consider him for the Asian Games. The decision acknowledges the recovery demands of the decathlon and keeps the national record holder in contention.

POR vs UZB Highlights: Undeniable Ronaldo, dominant Portugal hit 5 in routPortugal (POR) vs Uzbekistan (UZB) Highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026: Cristiano Ronaldo turned the spotlight into a stage, scoring twice as Portugal dismantled Uzbekistan in Houston. With Bruno Fernandes pulling the strings and set-pieces doing the damage, Roberto Martinez's side have looked every bit the contenders they promised to be.

New Zealand survive Scotland scare, remain alive in semis raceWomen's T20 Wolrd CUp 2026: Izzy Sharp and Brooke Halliday revived New Zealand to chase 132 after an early wobble. Darcey Carter's unbeaten 72 proved insufficient as Scotland's semi-final hopes ended, keeping New Zealand alive.

IPL trade buzz: KKR, Rajasthan open talks with MI over potential Hardik Pandya moveKolkata Knight Riders and Rajasthan Royals have approached Mumbai Indians over a potential Hardik Pandya trade. Rajasthan are eyeing a swap deal involving opening batter Yashasvi Jaiswal.

IND vs PAK hockey highlights: India beat Pakistan in 7-goal thrillerIndia (IND) vs Pakistan (PAK) hockey highlights, FIH Pro League: Pakistan bounced back with two late goals, but it was not enough as India won the seven-goal thriller 4-3 in London. Goals from Abhishek, Nilakanta Sharma, Sukhjeet Singh and Rajinder Singh sealed the win for India.

Rohit Sharma receives Padma Shri from President Murmu, wife Ritika beams with prideIndian cricketer Rohit Sharma, hockey star Savita Punia and tennis great Vijay Amritraj were among the sports stars who received their Padma Awards from President Droupadi Murmu on Tuesday in New Delhi.

Jordan knocked out of FIFA World Cup as resilient Algeria stage comeback victoryFIFA World Cup: Jordan became the first team from Group J to be knocked out of the FIFA World Cup. Despite taking the lead in the first half, Jordan let it slip in two crucial moments and ended up losing 2-1 to Algeria.

Sree Charani becomes No. 1 T20I bowler a year after making international debutSree Charani became the world's top-ranked T20I bowler just over a year after her India debut. The left-arm spinner has been in fine form in the ongoing Women's T20 World Cup campaign in England.

Argentina make life easier for Messi. Portugal make it harder for RonaldoArgentina have built a World Cup-winning ecosystem around Lionel Messi, allowing him to thrive at 39. Portugal, despite possessing immense talent, are still searching for the right formula to maximise Cristiano Ronaldo as another crucial test awaits against Uzbekistan.

Promising Suryansh Shedge replaces Nitish Reddy for Ireland, England T20I seriesSuryansh Shedge has replaced injured Nitish Kumar Reddy in India's T20I squad for the Ireland and England series. The Mumbai all-rounder earns a maiden call-up after strong India A and domestic performances.

Rishabh Pant takes huge pay cut for Delhi Capitals return, Kuldeep joins LSGIPL 2027: Lucknow Super Giants have mutually parted ways with Rishabh Pant after a disastrous Indian Premier League season. Pant is set to return to his former franchise Delhi Capitals, having taken a huge pay cut.

From 1 to 100: Messi fans across generations steal the spotlight in World CupLionel Messi fans from two very different generations stole the spotlight on Monday, when Argentina took the field against Austria in the FIFA World Cup. A 100-year-old grandma, and a child, barely one-year-old, were seen celebrating Messi and Argentina in their group-stage game.

ODI queens, T20 learners: South Africa hold up the mirror to Harmanpreet's IndiaSouth Africa delivered a timely reality check to India in Manchester. The six-wicket defeat exposed glaring shortcomings in Harmanpreet Kaur's side and raised fresh questions about their ability to translate ODI dominance into T20 success as the battle for the semi-finals heats up.

After Hardik Pandya, all-rounder Nitish Reddy ruled out of UK tourIndia have been dealt a big blow ahead of their tour of the United Kingdom. All-rounder Nitish Reddy has been ruled out of the Ireland and England series due to a quadriceps injury.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
